learning_outcomes.description: Personal health and hygiene

performance_criteria.description: Wear clean, smart and appropriate personal Protective Equipment.
Work safely at all times, complying with health and safety and other relevant regulations and guidelines.
Get any cuts, grazes and wounds Treated by the appropriate person.
Report illness and infection promptly to the appropriate Persons.
State own responsibility under the health and safety Act as it relates to own occupation.
State general rules on hygiene that must be followed.
Identify Personal Protective Equipment such as Head, Foot, Face, eye, Hand and Body protection as well as Regulatory Protection.
State the importance of maintaining good personal Hygiene.
Describe how to deal with cuts, grazes and wounds and why it is important to do so
Report any accidents or near accidents quickly and accurately to the proper person.
Follow health, hygiene and safely procedures during work
Practice emergency procedures during work
Follow organizational security Procedures
Ensure the disposal of waste and Pollution control with organic and inorganic waste disposal methods.
Follow sound and noise control and protection methods
Identify any hazards or potential hazards and deal with these correctly.
State where information about health and safety in your workplace can be obtained
Describe the types of hazards in the workplace that may occur and how to deal with them
State hazards that can be dealt with personally and those that should be reported to someone Else
State how to warn other people about hazards and why this is important.
Describe the types of emergencies that may happen in the workplace and how to deal with them

units.title: Communication system in a work environment

learning_outcomes.description: Non complex communicatio n system in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Use a simple verbal means to pass on necessary information
Use non-verbal means to pass on necessary information e.g. body language
Interpret symbols and signs appropriately

learning_outcomes.description: Source of information in a work Environment.

performance_criteria.description: Locate the source of information in an organization and work Environment.
Relate appropriately with source of information
Use the various information flow systems in a work environment
Use information to avoid challenges in a work situation
Report findings in accordance to Procedure in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Communicatio n n means in a work Environment.

performance_criteria.description: Locate the various communication equipment in the work environment
Use effectively the various communication equipment in a work Environment.
Pass information effectively to the right personnel
Pass information effectively using Symbols, signs and codes.
Obey instruction in line with ethics of The work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: work

performance_criteria.description: Describe team role and scope
Identify own role and responsibility within the team

learning_outcomes.description: Teams in the construction site

performance_criteria.description: Distinguish between various teams e.g. Masonry, Carpentry, Iron bending, Plumbing etc.

learning_outcomes.description: Roles of the various teams 2. 1 List the function of each team member 2. 2 Discuss the function of each team

performance_criteria.description: Differentiate between the function of each team

learning_outcomes.description: Requirement of various teams 3. 1 Identify materials and Tools required for each team member. 3. 2 Enumerate the function of tools and equipment required for each team 3. 3 Describe the method of carrying out activities for each team.
Benefit of team work.

performance_criteria.description: Recognize the sequence of various team activities
Differentiate between various time frame of each team activity 4. 3 Deliver quality output as a team

learning_outcomes.description: Materials, tools and equipment Required for setting out

performance_criteria.description: Distinguish the various types of materials, tools and equipment required in setting out.
Select the various types of materials, tools and equipment required in setting out.
Assemble the various types of materials, tools and equipment required in setting out.

learning_outcomes.description: Elementary building drawings

performance_criteria.description: Interpret Measuring unit and device.
Read and interpret various types of scales used.
Interpret simple sketches.
Convert Dimensions from Imperial to Metric Units.

learning_outcomes.description: Methods of setting out

performance_criteria.description: Explain the methods of setting out with examples
State various simple setting out tools

learning_outcomes.description: Set out simple masonry structure

performance_criteria.description: Identify and indicate site boundaries
Set right angled corners
Establish building line and set back
Check building lines for squareness and Compliance
Mark lines on profiles

units.title: Erection of masonry works: using appropriate

learning_outcomes.description: Tools, materials and equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify and use the tools and equipment for masonry works
Select materials for masonry work and apply them in construction.

learning_outcomes.description: Bricks, stone, block wall, concrete walls and floors

performance_criteria.description: Determine the different types of blocks/bricks/concrete wall and their sizes
Determine the appropriate courses to attend at a certain level
Explain the types of bonding in Construction
Create openings at appropriate levels and locations
Explain various mix ratios for blocks/bricks/concrete structures, thickness of joints and floors
Lay block, brick, concrete and floors to required standard

learning_outcomes.description: Techniques of laying natural stone structures

performance_criteria.description: Enumerate types of natural stone.
Determine the appropriate courses to attain at certain level
State purpose and condition under which stone work is Employed
Identify different types and purpose of wall thickness
Indicate the length at which double multi piers of wall will be introduced
Use expansion joint materials

learning_outcomes.description: Types of stairs /steps and method of construction

performance_criteria.description: Identify different types of staircase and steps
Describe the various part of a stair case e.g threads, riser, Landing.
Describe method
Explain method of fixing and placement of reinforcement.

learning_outcomes.description: Curing in masonry structures

performance_criteria.description: State the purpose of curing in masonry structures
Identify the materials required in curing masonry structures
Perform various types of curing techniques at required intervals

units.title: Rendering and Finishes according to specification

learning_outcomes.description: Materials for rendering and Finishes

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of rendering/finishes materials
Select required materials and tools in this Task
List the appropriate resources for plaster/POP/Floor finishes

learning_outcomes.description: Preparation of materials for rendering and Finishes

performance_criteria.description: Explain the mix ratio of the materials for the Task
Demonstrate mixing techniques
Explain timing of mix batches for the various task

learning_outcomes.description: Surfaces Preparation and application of rendering materials to surfaces/floors

performance_criteria.description: Clear surfaces of all dirt and debris.
Wet surface to be rendered
Place plaster/ floor thickness/level/gauge on surfaces
Apply rendering materials on surfaces to specifications
Position and secure fibrous plaster components/POP/Floors
Carry out proper finishes at edges
Protect all freshly finished surfaces from Damage
Describe the purpose of curing in rendering works and floors

learning_outcomes.description: Faulty or damage on masonry works

performance_criteria.description: Carry out periodic investigation of the Structure
Determine previous repair / rectification works on the structure
Identify minor causes of faults and damages.

learning_outcomes.description: Materials, tools, and equipment for work

performance_criteria.description: Identify various types of materials tools and equipment
Select various types of materials tools and equipment

learning_outcomes.description: Faulty or damage portion removal

performance_criteria.description: Identify and select various necessary tools and equipment for the work
Determine the procedures for the removal of damaged portion
Remove damaged portions using appropriate safety measures.

learning_outcomes.description: Repair /Replace faulty portion of the structures Carry out periodic LO (Learning outcome)

performance_criteria.description: Identify the materials required
Select and prepare the material required
Repair/replace damaged portion Criteria:- Ref Page number

learning_outcomes.description: Finish of repaired/replaced portion of work

performance_criteria.description: Identify and select required materials for the works
Prepare materials for all finishing work and cure the repaired and replaced portion of work

learning_outcomes.description: Dismantle /remove/lifting/ Lowering Equipment Level Purpose: This qualification is for the Senior Mason, who needs to be familiar with assistant mason and masons job descriptions as well as coordinate and carryout advanced masonry works. supervise Assistant Mason and Mason and carry out advanced masonry work. QUALIFICATION SUMMARY UNIT 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 09 10

performance_criteria.description: Carry out dismantling procedures
Dismantle and store used materials /equipment
Ensure proper house keeping

Total learning hours 1000 + Guided learning hours Independent learning hours Work-based learning hours 400 600 + (based on training needs) CREDI T VALUE REF NO GLH UNIT ` REMARKS CONMS001L3 Health, Safety and Environment Mandatory CONMS002L3 30 Communication system in a work environment CONMS003L3 Team Work in Construction Environment CONMS004L3 Special structures 9 CONMS005L3 90 Masonry Work in high-rise complex structures elements and techniques in construction CONMS006L3 Plastering on Internal/External surfaces of masonry structures and POP works CONMS007L3 Plan, organise and estimate works to meet required standard CONMS008L3 Water proofing work on masonry structures CONMS009L3 Decorative Optional Moulding Installation CONMS010L3 Terrazo Flooring
